name: @data
doc:
@data <head> <variants>

Create a new algebraic data type (also known as a sum type) with the given head and variants.

name: data_type
doc: Return the data type of the given variant type.

name: data_type_name
doc: Return the data type name of the given variant.

name: is_data_type
doc: Check if the given object is a variant of a algebraic data type.

name: is_variant_type
doc: Check if the given object is a variant type.

name: isa_variant
doc: Check if the given variant is an instance of the given variant type.

name: show_variant
doc:
Print the variant to the given IO stream in multiple lines.

Print the variant to the given IO stream in a single line.

name: storage_types
doc:
Return the storage types of the data type.

!!! note
    This method is used by the pattern matching system to extract the field of the variant.

name: variant_fieldnames
doc: Return the field names of the variant.

name: variant_fieldtypes
doc: Return the field types of the variant.

name: variant_getfield
doc:
Known the variant type `tag`, return the field of the variant by field name or index.

!!! note
    This method is used by the pattern matching system to extract the field of the variant.
    It is not intended to be used directly. Most of cases, you can use the `x.field` syntax
    to extract the field of the variant. However, Julia compiler is not able to infer the
    type of the variant usually, so if you care about performance, you may want to use this
    method in combine with [`variant_storage`](@ref).

name: variant_kind
doc: Return the kind of the variant, can be `Singleton`, `Anonymous`, or `Named`.

name: variant_name
doc: Return the name of the variant.

name: variant_nfields
doc: Return the number of fields of the variant.

name: variant_storage
doc: Return the storage object of the variant.

name: variant_storage_type
doc: Return the storage type of the variant.

name: variant_type
doc: Return the variant type of the given variant.

name: variants
doc:
Return a tuple of variants of the given data type.

Return a tuple of variants of the given data type.
